raw_recent_text: Keep it up!
You've survived 4 days!
Yesterday, at this time, you had $75.
Since then, you've managed to lose $16.
That brings you to a grand total of $59!
You're in the 'ramen noodle' tax bracket.
Your efforts haven't gone unnoticed. They're just not always mentioned.
Take a moment to reflect on this:
"Would you believe that my neighbor came ringing my doorbell at 2:00 this morning? Luckily for him, I was still up playing bagpipes."
Press a key to continue: ~ ~ ~ Morning, Day 5 ~ ~ ~  True
You slept so hard you forgot where you were for a second. That's the good kind of confusion.
You drive your car to a gas station for supplies. Just snacks. Maybe some coffee.
You're browsing the chips when the door slams open.
"EVERYBODY ON THE GROUND! NOW!"
A man with a shotgun. Ski mask. Shaking hands. This is really happening.
The cashier freezes. A mother clutches her child. An old man drops his coffee.
The robber swings the gun around, wild-eyed.

available_menu_options: No effort. No suspense. Just the house being the house.
available_routes: No effort. No suspense. Just the house being the house.

metadata.recent_lower:
you wake up smiling. when's the last time that happened? you can't remember, but it happened today.
your phone buzzes from the passenger seat. you have a text from an unknown number.
"grandma passed away. the funeral is saturday."
you stare at your phone for a long moment, then remember you don't recognize this number at all.
you type back: "wrong number, buddy."
they reply: "omg i'm so sorry! here's $10 for the trouble via cash app!"
wait, that actually worked? you got $10!
your new balance is $75 + $10 = $85
whiskers yawns, showing every single tiny fang. good morning to you too.
having whiskers by your side makes the world a little less cruel.
you wake up to scratching noises. something is on your car.
you look up. a family of raccoons is making themselves at home on your roof.
mom raccoon. dad raccoon. three baby raccoons. all staring at you through the windshield with those little bandit masks.
you honk the horn. they don't flinch. you honk again. the babies yawn.
you stare at each other for a full minute. they're not leaving. this is their car now.
you go back to sleep. in the morning, they've left a dead mouse on your hood. a gift? a threat? both?
as the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more blackjack.
ready to play? the question is always yes, isn't it?
you have $85
your first card is a three of hearts
the dealer's first card is an ace of spades
your second card is a seven of spades
the dealer's second card is a jack of spades
the dealer's hand has a value of 21 ;)
your hand has a value of 10
natural 21 for the house. the dealer doesn't even celebrate. this is tuesday for him.
you had $85 and lost your bet of $10
your new balance is $85 - $10 = $75
your first card is a two of spades
the dealer's first card is a five of hearts
your second card is a nine of spades
the dealer's second card is face down
as of now, the dealer's hand has a known value of 5
your hand has a value of 11
your next card is a two of clubs
your hand has a value of 13
the dealer's second card is a six of hearts
the dealer's hand has a value of 11
the dealer's hand has a value under 17 so they hit
the dealer's next card is a king of diamonds
the dealer's hand has a value of 21
21. no effort. no suspense. just the house being the house.
you had $75 and lost your bet of $9
your new balance is $75 - $9 = $66
your first card is a seven of diamonds
the dealer's first card is an eight of diamonds
your second card is a seven of clubs
the dealer's second card is face down
as of now, the dealer's hand has a known value of 8
your hand has a value of 14
your next card is a nine of hearts
your hand has a value of 23
you busted. the dealer barely glances at your cards. he already knew.
you had $66 and lost your bet of $7
your new balance is $66 - $7 = $59
after playing a few rounds of blackjack, the dealer points to the door.
without questioning his word, and with your winnings in hand, you scurry to the door, eager to get some sleep.
making it back to your car, ditched on the side of the road, you lay down, and close your eyes. it's time to rest.

raw_recent_text: You've survived 9 days!
Yesterday, at this time, you had $60.
Since then, you've managed to lose $18.
That brings you to a grand total of $42!
You're basically living paycheck to paycheck. Except there's no paycheck.
Congrats on all the hard work so far. It's nice to know you're still alive.
I read this once in a magazine:
"Never do anything half-heartedly; always use your full heart, even if it's misguided, wrong, or downright bizarre. Full-hearted mistakes make the best stories."
Press a key to continue: ~ ~ ~ Morning, Day 10 ~ ~ ~  True
Your current sanity: 3%
You stretch out and feel every vertebra in your spine crack in the most satisfying way. Today is going to be good.
Through your windshield, there's a car parked right in front of you. That wasn't there before.
Confused, and dazed, you sit up. As you open the door and get out of your car,
you notice a man, in a bright red suit, peering into your trunk.
The man sees you, and walks up to you.
"You. You're awake. Good. You know that you aren't supposed to be here? "
"This isn't a spot for people to live. This is a road for people to drive. I hope you know this."
"Do you know this?"
